name: Centennial Funding
description_short: Centennial Funding is a debt consolidation marketplace that matches borrowers with personal loans to consolidate debt, lower payments, and refinance high-interest obligations.
description_long:
Centennial Funding operates as a debt consolidation and personal loan matching service based in Denver, Colorado. The company positions itself as an alternative to traditional lending by offering a simplified application process that takes less than 60 seconds to complete. Rather than lending directly, Centennial Funding acts as a marketplace that connects borrowers with loan providers offering debt consolidation, personal loans, and other financing products.

The company offers a broad range of financing solutions including debt consolidation loans, personal loans, home loans, auto loans, business loans, credit card refinancing, medical expense loans, student loans, and large purchase financing. Their primary value proposition centers on consolidating multiple high-interest debts into a single fixed-rate loan with lower monthly payments and interest rates. The company emphasizes quick approvals, no prepayment penalties, and customized plans tailored to individual credit profiles. Applications are matched against their network of providers within seconds using 256-bit encryption for data security.

Centennial Funding differentiates itself through its speed of service, claiming sub-60-second applications and rapid matching with lenders. The company employs Finance Managers available by phone to answer questions and guide borrowers through the enrollment process, with in-person meetings available in Denver to review finalized plans. They offer a referral program providing $500 rebates to enrolled customers who refer friends and $100 cash to referred customers with no existing debt.

As a marketplace rather than a direct lender, Centennial Funding's actual loan terms, interest rates, and approval standards depend entirely on the providers in their network. The website makes broad claims about helping borrowers avoid being "turned down for loans" and offering "low interest rates," but provides no specific APR ranges, borrowing limits, credit score requirements, or approval rates. The company's Denver-based location may limit service availability or in-person support for borrowers outside Colorado.

When evaluating debt relief companies, consumers should compare settlement programs against alternatives like debt consolidation loans, which combine multiple debts into a single fixed-rate payment. Credit counseling through nonprofit agencies offers free budgeting help without impacting credit scores. For those whose credit has already been damaged, credit repair services can address inaccurate negative items on reports. Personal loans for bad credit may provide funds for debt payoff at lower rates than credit cards, and credit monitoring services help track progress throughout the recovery process. Consolidating high-interest balances into a single installment loan with a fixed rate can reduce total interest paid and simplify monthly budgeting.

pricing.guarantee_details: Contact provider for current pricing and guarantee details.

pros: Quick application process taking less than 60 seconds to compare offers
Access to multiple lender offers through a single application rather than shopping individually
No prepayment penalties, allowing borrowers to pay off debt faster without additional costs
Finance Manager phone support and in-person meetings available in Denver to review plans
256-bit encryption employed to protect sensitive financial information during application
Broad range of loan types offered including debt consolidation, personal, auto, home, business, and medical loans
$500 referral rebates for enrolled customers who refer others
cons: No specific APR ranges, interest rates, or borrowing limits disclosed on website—actual terms depend on matched lenders
No credit score requirements or approval rate information provided; claims about loan approvals are unsubstantiated
Acts as a marketplace matching service rather than direct lender, so actual loan quality and terms vary by provider
Denver-based company with in-person meetings only available in Colorado, limiting convenience for out-of-state borrowers
Vague language about being the 'best debt consolidation company' and helping borrowers avoid loan rejections without supporting data
best_for: Borrowers with multiple high-interest debts seeking to consolidate into a single lower-rate loan
Consumers wanting to compare multiple personal loan offers quickly without shopping multiple lenders individually
Colorado residents who can benefit from in-person Finance Manager consultations in Denver
Borrowers seeking a range of loan types (auto, home, business, medical) through a single application platform
diagnosis: Centennial Funding is best for borrowers seeking to consolidate multiple debts or compare personal loan offers quickly through a single application. The main caveat is that as a marketplace matching service rather than direct lender, actual loan terms, APRs, and approval standards are determined by their provider network—the website provides no transparency on typical rates, requirements, or success rates, making it difficult to assess whether matched offers will actually be competitive or result in approval.
services: Debt consolidation loans combining multiple debts into single fixed-rate loans
Personal loans for cash needs at competitive rates
Auto loan matching for vehicle purchases
Business loan financing and consultation with business finance advisors
Credit card refinancing consolidating high-interest balances into fixed-rate loans
Medical expense loans to cover healthcare costs
Student loan financing for education expenses
Large purchase financing for planned expenditures
Rapid loan matching within seconds of application submission
Finance Manager phone support to answer questions and guide enrollment
In-person plan review meetings in Denver to finalize loan terms
typical_results_timeline: Results vary by individual situation. Contact the provider to discuss expected timelines for your specific needs.
